articles.title: Production-Ready AI Agents: 5 Lessons from Refactoring a Monolith
articles.snippet: The blog post outlines the transition of a brittle sales research prototype into a robust production agent using Google’s Agent Development Kit (ADK). articles.ai_summary: The blog discusses how a sales research prototype was transformed into a reliable production AI agent using Google's Agent Development Kit, enhancing scalability and transparency. articles.why_it_matters: Improves understanding of building robust AI systems for real-world applications.

articles.title: MixAtlas: Uncertainty-aware Data Mixture Optimization for Multimodal LLM Midtraining
articles.snippet:
This paper was accepted at the Workshop on Navigating and Addressing Data Problems for Foundation Models (NADPFM) at ICLR 2026.
Principled domain reweighting can substantially improve sample efficiency and downstream generalization; however, data-mixture optimization for multimodal pretraining remains underexplored. Current multimodal training recipes tune mixtures from only a single perspective such as data format or task type. We introduce MixAtlas, a principled framework for compute-efficient
articles.ai_summary: The paper discusses MixAtlas, a new framework for optimizing data mixtures in multimodal training, which could enhance sample efficiency and generalization. This research addresses a gap in current multimodal pretraining methods.

articles.ai_summary: The paper discusses the limitations of Small Language Models (SLMs) in retaining factual knowledge due to their parameter size. It suggests that allowing SLMs to access external sources can mitigate inaccuracies in their outputs.

articles.title: Governance-Aware Agent Telemetry for Closed-Loop Enforcement in Multi-Agent AI Systems
articles.snippet: Enterprise multi-agent AI systems produce thousands of inter-agent interactions per hour, yet existing observability tools capture these dependencies without enforcing anything. OpenTelemetry and Langfuse collect telemetry but treat governance as a downstream analytics concern, not a real-time enforcement target. The result is an “observe-but-do-not-act” gap where policy violations are detected only after damage is done. We present Governance-Aware Agent Telemetry (GAAT), a reference architectur
articles.ai_summary: The article introduces Governance-Aware Agent Telemetry (GAAT), a new framework aimed at real-time enforcement of policies in multi-agent AI systems, addressing the gap in current observability tools.
articles.why_it_matters: Enhances governance and compliance in complex AI systems, reducing risks of policy violations.
